understanding.goal_understanding: The goal was to complete each KA59 level by moving and/or switching between selectable pieces so that every goal tile marked with a '+' is occupied by a selectable piece at the same time, within the available step budget; achieving WIN on the final level completes the run.
understanding.mechanics_understanding: Movement occurs on a grid using 3-pixel cell increments: MOVE_* attempts to shift the currently selected piece by one cell (e.g., x/y changes by ±3). Walls/boundaries can block movement, and the 'movement.*_blocked' flags are only hints (adjacent-object detection) rather than guaranteed failures. Some adjacent objects (e.g., blocks) can be pushable, moving along when the selected piece moves into them if space allows; otherwise the move is blocked. If multiple selectables exist, control is changed with CLICK by providing the exact pixel/grid coordinate of the target selectable's position, after which subsequent MOVE_* actions affect that newly selected piece.

understanding.goal_understanding: The goal was to complete each KA59 level by moving and/or switching between selectable pieces so that every goal tile marked with a '+' is occupied by a selectable piece at the same time, within the available step budget; achieving WIN on the final level completes the run.
understanding.mechanics_understanding: Movement occurs on a grid using 3-pixel cell increments: MOVE_* attempts to shift the currently selected piece by one cell (e.g., x/y changes by ±3). Walls/boundaries can block movement, and the 'movement.*_blocked' flags are only hints (adjacent-object detection) rather than guaranteed failures. Some adjacent objects (e.g., blocks) can be pushable, moving along when the selected piece moves into them if space allows; otherwise the move is blocked. If multiple selectables exist, control is changed with CLICK by providing the exact pixel/grid coordinate of the target selectable's position, after which subsequent MOVE_* actions affect that newly selected piece.
